textAreas.text:
Hawai'i Road Data
from state geoportal

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text:
Closest Facility analysis Layer w/ No Restriction.. 
Load gravity line endpoints as facilities and load potential NPS points as incidents

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Run to generate flow lines from parcels to main roads

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Select sinks that have flow lines going to them (potential NPS)

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Nodes where 3+ or 1 street segments meet

textAreas.text:
Only routes that conform to
Restriction 1 will be created; Slopes > 1% will act as blockers

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Gravity sewer lines from homes to main road

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Service Area analysis; Load nodes as facilities; Set Restriction 2; Set distance very short like 1 meter and run solver

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Slope multiplied by -1 for flipped segments; Start and end elev values switched

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Restriction 2: Forward travel allowed for negative slope only (downhill)

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text:
Potential
Neighborhood Pump Stations

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: This selects the subset of roadway endpoints that can be considered major roads as opposed to neighborhood streets. We are using HPMS ONLY as a selector because it doesn't match Hawai'i roads exactly hence the 10m tolerance

textAreas.label: Placeholder
textAreas.text: Type something

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Start elev, end elev, slope calculated  for all road segments

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Road segments all have direction based on how they were drawn originally. Slopes can be positive, 0, or negative depending on direction and elev values

textAreas.label: Placeholder
textAreas.text: Type something

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: The roads are doubled because restrictions apply in the direction a road is drawn. Doubling up roads allows each direction to have a separate restriction. Eg. A given segment is uphill one way and downhill the other. Flow can go downhill but not uphill; But a shallow upslope or flat street can allow flow both ways because sewer lines have their own slope underground

textAreas.label: Placeholder
textAreas.text: Type something

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Original and flipped roads combined into one layer

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: All Parcels

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Parcel Centroids

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: All roads doubled, Flip Line direction of new copy

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Select nodes that didn't generate ANY outward flow lines. These are terminal points or sinks within the neighborhood

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Restriction 1: Forward travel allowed for negative slope, 0 slope, or +1% positive slope

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Neighborhood Pressure Lines

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Network Analysis Layer created from combined road layer

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text:
Endpoint = start and end of each
roadway segment. In Hawai'i statewide
data the segments are cut at intersections 
so this is a logical way to break up roads
and calculate slopes. Other places may
have less clean data

Where 2 road segments meet aren't really nodes,
they could just be points along straight roadways

textAreas.text:
Closest Facility analysis Layer w/ Restriction 1... 
Load sinks as facilities and load non-gravity parcel points as incidents

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Run to generate lines that represent outward flow from each node

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Select road endpoints that are within 10 meters of HPMS

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Load major road endpoints as Facilities; Load parcel centroids as Incidents; Snap to 0m

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: Select already snapped snapped parcel points that do NOT have gravity routes to main road

textAreas.label: Text
textAreas.text: These are remainder homes that don't have a direct gravity route to the main road. They will need their own gravity routes down to local concentration points and then pumping back uphill to reach gravity routes
